title: Excessive granting of GCP IAM permissions is security-sensitive
description: Granting excessive IAM permissions in GCP increases the attack surface and risk of privilege escalation. Users and service accounts should be granted only the permissions necessary for their roles (principle of least privilege). Overly broad permissions can lead to data breaches and unauthorized actions.
message: IAM permissions are too broad. Restrict to least privilege required for the role.
impact: Excessive permissions can be exploited by attackers or misused by insiders, leading to data loss, service disruption, or environment compromise.
recommendation: Review IAM policies and roles. Grant only the minimum permissions required. Use predefined roles where possible and audit custom roles regularly.

noncompliant.description: User is granted the 'owner' role, which provides full administrative access.

compliant.description: User is granted only the 'viewer' role, following least privilege.

why_is_this_an_issue: Excessive permissions increase the risk of accidental or malicious actions that can compromise security and compliance.
how_to_fix: Audit all IAM policies and roles. Remove unnecessary permissions and enforce least privilege. Monitor for changes to IAM configurations.
